title: Adult Basic Education - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Education > Adult Basic Education 27 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Hispanic/Latino Community ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a holistic education program for Latina women working towards a GED, including coursework in English Language Development (ELD), mathematics, grammar, parenting, and an 18-week seminar focused on conflict resolution (ESPERE). The Early Childhood Education program provides preschool and child care for participants' children. 2030 Main Street Suite A Forest Grove, OR 97116 Eligibility: Low-income Spanish-speaking women and their children, ages 6 weeks-5 years Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Classes are held 5 days per week for a full year Email (503) 992-0078 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers community college education including university transfer degrees, career and technical education certificates and degrees, adult basic skills and GED preparation, college readiness, workforce training, continuing and community education, and lifelong learning opportunities. The Adult Basic Education (ABE) program is for individuals with or without a high school diploma or GED to help prepare for college-level coursework, advance in their careers, or achieve personal milestones by strengthening essential reading, writing, and math. Campuses are located in Pendleton (main campus), Hermiston, Milton-Freewater, Baker City, Boardman, La Grande, and John Day. 2411 NW Carden Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Must be age 16 and not enrolled in school. Students under age 18 must have a release from school district in which they reside before attempting to pass the GED test. Hours: Schedule varies Email (541) 276-1260 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Campuses are located in Bend, Redmond, Madras, and Prineville. 2600 NW College Way Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 383-7700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY CENTRO DE PROSPERIDAD HILLSBORO ADULT BASIC EDUCATION, JOB SEARCH, AND BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers educational programs to individuals who are seeking to improve employability. Programs include GED tutoring and computer literacy. Also assists clients in attaining job readiness through job application assistance, interview preparation, resume building and locating internship and on the job training (OJT) opportunities. Business development services are offered at no cost to those who wish to start their own business and to existing small business owners who wish to improve business practices. 265 SE Oak Street Suite A Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon Email (503) 368-9895 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CHEMEKETA COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and ESL classes. Main campuses are located in Salem and McMinnville. 4000 Lancaster Drive NE Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Class times vary Email (503) 399-5000 Get Directions Visit Website More Campus locations in Astoria and Seaside. 1651 Lexington Avenue Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 338-2411 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE THE DALLES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 400 E Scenic Drive Building 2, Room 2.035 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-6pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 506-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 7390 S 6th Street Klamath Falls, OR 97603 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 882-3521 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LANE COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers transfer degrees, 40+ career technical programs, and a variety of non-credit courses. Academic counseling and Career Information System (CIS), which lists job occupations of interest are offered as well. Provides career planning, academic, and personal advising, adult basic education, various instructional programs, disability services, telecourses, and distance learning. 4000 E 30th Avenue Building 24 Eugene, OR 97405 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 463-3000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN BENTON COMMUNITY COLLEGE GED AND ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers classes to help native and non-native speakers improve their math, reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills. Classes include Adult Basic Education, GED preparation, Spanish GED preparation, skill improvement, and English as a Second Language. The main campus is in Albany with centers in Corvallis, Lebanon, and Sweet Home. 6500 Pacific Boulevard SW Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Varies by location, visit website or call for information on current term offerings Email (541) 917-4710 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LITERACY COUNCIL FOR JOSEPHINE COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one tutoring. Students learn to read, write, learn English as a second language, improve basic math skills, and prepare for the citizenship test. Assists to improve job skills, pass the written driver's exam, fill out job applications and other forms, read medicine labels and signs, and learn to use a computer in an educational capacity. Offers a specialized program designed to help those with the learning disability dyslexia learn to read, write, and spell. Also helps students qualify for enrollment at Rogue Community College (RCC) and assists them in preparing for their GED in language arts, math, science, and social studies. 132 NE B Street Suite 23 Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Age 18 and older Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 472-0355 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and ESL classes. 26000 SE Stark Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Class times vary (503) 491-6422 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COAST COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Main campus is in Newport with locations in Lincoln City and Waldport. 400 SE College Way Newport, OR 97366 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 867-8501 Get pre-college education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language(ESL) classes. Individual campuses are located in a wide variety of cities within the service area. 3345 Redwood Highway Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 956-7500 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM MEDFORD LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Program that provides adult English as a Second Language (ESL) instruction and adult basic education for parents. Children participate in the program with their parents by attending developmentally appropriate classes and getting homework help and Spanish reading support. 1001 Beall Lane Central Point, OR 97502 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-15 Hours: Tuesday/Thursday 5:30pm-8pm (541) 734-5150 1049 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 1988 Newmark Avenue Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 888-2525 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE CURRY CAMPUS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. 96082 Lone Ranch Parkway Brookings, OR 97415 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 813-1667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. Campuses are located in Tillamook, Rockaway Beach, and Cloverdale. 4301 Third Street Tillamook, OR 97141 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm (503) 842-8222 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TREASURE VALLEY COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers a variety of educational, vocational, college credit, and developmental courses, including adult basic education, GED test instruction, continuing education opportunities, and English as a Second Language classes. The main campus is located in Ontario, Oregon, with a center in Caldwell, Idaho. 650 College Boulevard Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 881-8822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UMPQUA COMMUNITY COLLEGE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Umpqua Community College's Adult Basic Skills Development program helps adults who want to get their GED, improve their reading and math skills, prepare for college or a career, and learn English as a Second Language (ESL). Offers classes for people who have earned their diploma or GED and want to improve their basic education skills. Classes are held in various areas, including Roseburg, Glide, and Tri City/Myrtle Creek. ESL classes are held in Roseburg and Tri City/Myrtle Creek. Free tutoring program, school-to-career program, and several computer labs also availble to students. 1140 Umpqua College Road Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Age 16 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 440-4600 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ADELANTE MUJERES ADULT EDUCATION PROGRAM BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE BLUE MOUNTAIN COMMUNITY COLLEGE CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE CENTRAL OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE CENTRO CULTURAL OF WASHINGTON COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION, JOB SEARCH, AND BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT CHEMEKETA COMMUNITY COLLEGE CHEMEKETA COMMUNITY COLLEGE CLACKAMAS COMMUNITY COLLEGE CLACKAMAS COMMUNITY COLLEGE OREGON CITY CAMPUS CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE CLATSOP COMMUNITY COLLEGE COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE COLUMBIA GORGE COMMUNITY COLLEGE THE DALLES KLAMATH COMMUNITY COLLEGE KLAMATH COMMUNITY COLLEGE LANE COMMUNITY COLLEGE LANE COMMUNITY COLLEGE LINN BENTON COMMUNITY COLLEGE GED AND ADULT BASIC EDUCATION LITERACY COUNCIL FOR JOSEPHINE COUNTY ADULT BASIC EDUCATION MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE MT HOOD COMMUNITY COLLEGE OREGON COAST COMMUNITY COLLEGE OREGON COAST COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE CASCADE CAMPUS PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE ROCK CREEK CAMPUS PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHEAST CAMPUS PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE PORTLAND COMMUNITY COLLEGE SYLVANIA CAMPUS ROGUE COMMUNITY COLLEGE ROGUE COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHERN OREGON HEAD START LISTO FAMILY LITERACY PROGRAM SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE SOUTHWESTERN OREGON COMMUNITY COLLEGE CURRY CAMPUS TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE TILLAMOOK BAY COMMUNITY COLLEGE TREASURE VALLEY COMMUNITY COLLEGE TREASURE VALLEY COMMUNITY COLLEGE UMPQUA COMMUNITY COLLEGE UMPQUA COMMUNITY COLLEGE
